templates.name: Customer Filed Support Case
templates.template: Support case '{case_number}' (Severity {severity}) filed by '{customer_name}' for '{support_product_name}': '{title}'. Created: {created_date_time}. State: {state}.
templates.extracted_when: A support case exists in the dataset for the relevant time window — direct signal of customer impact, regardless of severity or escalation status.

templates.name: Customer Raised CritSit
templates.template: {case_count} support case(s) flagged as CritSit for '{support_product_name}' by '{customer_name}': [{case_numbers}]. First case created: {earliest_case_date}. CritSit indicates a critical business-impacting situation requiring immediate attention.
templates.extracted_when: One or more cases flagged as CritSit — critical business impact requiring immediate attention.

templates.name: Customer Escalated Support Case
templates.template: Support case '{case_number}' for '{support_product_name}' was escalated (initial severity {initial_severity} → current severity {severity}): '{title}'. Customer '{customer_name}'. The severity increase post-filing indicates the issue is more serious than initially assessed.
templates.extracted_when: Case severity increased post-filing — issue is worse than initially assessed.

templates.name: Multiple Cases Same Product
templates.template: {case_count} support cases filed for '{support_product_name}' by customer '{customer_name}'. Max severity: {max_severity}. Multiple cases for the same product suggest a systemic product issue rather than an isolated incident.
templates.extracted_when: Multiple support cases exist for the same product — systemic product issue rather than an isolated incident.

templates.name: Multiple Customers Filed Support Requests
templates.template: {distinct_customer_count} distinct customers have filed support cases for '{support_product_name}': {customer_list}. Total cases: {total_case_count}. Max severity: {max_severity}. CritSit cases: {crit_sit_count}. Regions: {region_list}. This cross-customer pattern confirms a widespread service problem.
templates.extracted_when: 2+ distinct customers filed SRs for the same product — confirms the issue is widespread, not isolated to a single customer.

templates.name: Multiple Customer CritSits
templates.template: {distinct_crit_sit_customer_count} distinct customers have CritSit-flagged cases for '{support_product_name}': {crit_sit_customer_list}. Total CritSit cases: {crit_sit_count}. Regions: {crit_sit_region_list}. Multiple customers experiencing critical situations for the same product indicates a severe, widespread platform failure requiring immediate coordinated response.
templates.extracted_when: Multiple customers raised CritSits for the same product — smoking-gun signal of critical platform failure requiring immediate coordinated response.
